normal_scan: A scan displaying uniform echotexture with subtle posterior acoustic enhancement, mimicking normal breast tissue while harboring a benign cystic structure.
An image with smooth, well-defined hypoechoic regions resembling normal lobular patterns but concealing a fibroadenoma with homogeneous internal echoes.
A low-resolution scan where poor focusing creates a deceptively uniform appearance, masking small focal lesions that disrupt normal breast tissue architecture.
Post-biopsy scan displaying mild edema and scattered echogenic foci, simulating normal adipose tissue distribution without apparent pathological features.
A scan with subtle microcalcifications obscured by dense glandular tissue, presenting with a deceptively normal homogeneous echotexture despite underlying malignant potential.
benign_tumor: A malignant tumor with smooth, well-defined margins and homogeneous echotexture typically associated with benign lesions but exhibiting subtle microcalcifications.
An irregularly shaped mass with partially obscured margins mimicking benign encapsulation, yet displaying malignant vascular patterns on Doppler imaging.
A complex cystic lesion with minimal internal debris and posterior acoustic enhancement, closely resembling a solid benign tumor in grayscale ultrasound.
A large, round hypoechoic mass with gentle lobulations and absent posterior shadowing, often misclassified as a benign fibroadenoma despite malignant histology.
A focal asymmetry with mild architectural distortion and indistinct margins that intermittently appear circumscribed, simulating a benign tumor's morphology.
malignant_tumor: A well-circumscribed, oval hypoechoic lesion with smooth margins and homogeneous internal echoes, mimicking malignancy due to mild posterior acoustic enhancement.
A complex cystic mass with thick septations and low-level internal echoes, mistaken for a solid tumor despite lacking Doppler vascularity or spiculated borders.
An inflammatory abscess with irregular margins, surrounding edema, and central necrosis, resembling malignancy but associated with pain and fever.
A post-biopsy hematoma with heterogeneous echotexture, irregular shape, and peripheral shadowing, falsely suggesting aggressive growth patterns.
A benign fibroadenoma with lobulated contours and mild microcalcifications, overlapping with malignant features like coarse calcifications or architectural distortion.
